system.description:
You have dug deep into the history of your bonded artifact, learning as much as you can about the source of your abilities, and, as is often the case, discovered that knowledge is power. You gain the following benefits:

  • Your Strength, Dexterity, or wielder spellcasting ability score increases by +1, to a maximum of 20.

  • Your bonded artifact is effectively weightless for you when determining your encumbrance. This does not and cannot reduce the weight of anything except the artifact itself, and only affects you. For all other creatures and in all other circumstances, without exception, your artifact weighs its normal weight.

  • You gain proficiency with either Arcana or History. If you are already proficient with both, choose one to gain an expertise die.

  • You can cast a wielder spell as a ritual if that spell has the ritual tag and you know the spell. You learn two ritual spells of first level from the wizard list. They count as wielder spells for you and do not count against your spells known.

  • You learn your choice of either the @UUID[Compendium.a5e.a5e-maneuvers.Item.e2k7hxnsl3sdrldt]{Purge Magic} or @UUID[Compendium.a5e.a5e-maneuvers.Item.zj2kp2v57pwfq2u7]{Anticipate Spell} maneuvers. The new maneuver does not count against your maneuvers known.
system.prerequisite: 4 levels in wielder, no levels in non-wielder classes at the time of feat acquisition
